LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MARIO GIOVANNETTI'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND SUBMITTED BY ALL SIDES PRESENT. THE COURT CONFIRMS THE TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO WHETHER DEFENDANT MARIO GIOVANNETTI WAS A CAUSE OF THE ACCIDENT. ANDREW ISIP'S DEPOSITION TESTIMONY IS EQUIVOCAL. AT CERTAIN POINTS ISIP SAID DEFENDANT'S TRUCK ENTERED HIS LANE ON THE FREEWAY. (ISIP DEP. PP. 29, 64-65.) AT OTHER POINTS ISIP INDICATED THE TRUCK MAY NOT HAVE ENTERED HIS LANE. (ID. AT P. 65.) A TRIER OF FACT NEEDS TO RESOLVE THE FACTUAL DISPUTE. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: REYES HUNTER, CSR #6576
MINI MINUTES FOR AUG-08-2016 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-25-2017 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Nov-21-2016 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jan-31-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement
Off Calendar Oct-26-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Defendants Xiao Dai and Yuting Wu's motion for determination of good faith settlement is granted. No opposition filed. The moving party is to provide a form of order.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-08-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Katie McIver of Harrison Personal Injury Lawyers Inc. (408-512-3600) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Jacy C. Dardine of McNamara Ney Beatty et al LLP (925-939-5330) appears as counsel for defendant/cross-defendant/cross-complainant/moving/opposing party, Mario Giovannetti

Attorney, James Huan Ly of Edrington Schirmer & Murphy (925-827-3300) appears as counsel for defendant/opposing party, Joshua Isip

The cause is argued and submitted by all sides present. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT MARIO GIOVANNETTI'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is DENIED. There is a triable issue of material fact as to whether defendant Mario Giovannetti was a cause of the accident. Andrew Isip's deposition testimony is equivocal. At certain points Isip said defendant's truck entered his lane on the freeway. (Isip Dep. pp. 29, 64-65.) At other points Isip indicated the truck may not have entered his lane. (Id. at p. 65.) A trier of fact needs to resolve the factual dispute. Prevailing party to submit a [proposed] written order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR #6576, Bus. Tel: 530-339-5023, email: rhunter@sftc.org. (D302)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Dec-24-2015 Set For Trial On Nov-28-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-20-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. KATIE MELVER, ESQ. of Harris Personal Injury Lawyers, Inc., Tel No. 415-495-0440 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. JAMES HUAN LY, ESQ. of Edrington, Schirmer & Murphy LLP, Tel No. 925-827-3300 appearing for Defendant Joshua Isip.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being heard by the pro tem judge. Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court modifies its tentative ruling, and orders as follows: Having read and considered the motion, including the memorandum of points and authorities, all timely submitted admissible evidence, IT IS ORDERED THAT Defendant Joshua Andrew Isip's Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses And Request For Monetary Sanctions is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. Defense counsel shall prepare and submit a proposed order for signature in compliance with CRC 3.1312, reflecting the modifications to the tentative ruling discussed at hearing. Judge Pro Tem: Naomi Gray, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/JPT)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-09-2015 Set For Trial On Jul-05-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
